item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 10)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine begin 3-day ceasefire as tensions ramp up between U.S. and Iran
item.description: President Trump said Friday that fighting would halt between Russia and Ukraine for three days. Meanwhile, the U.S. is awaiting Iran's response to a peace proposal.

item.title: WHO director says he will personally oversee hantavirus cruise evacuation
item.description: The MV Hondius is expected to reach the Canary Islands early Sunday. Passengers are set to be carefully removed hours after docking.

item.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán's rule
item.description: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party defeated Orbán's nationalist-populist Fidesz in a stunning blow last month.

item.title: Frontier plane fatally hits pedestrian on Denver runway
item.description: A Frontier plane struck a pedestrian on a runway in Denver late Friday night, according to the airline and Denver International Airport. Pilots were forced to cancel the takeoff to Los Angeles after smoke was reported in the cabin and the pedestrian was killed, the airport also said.

item.title: Eye Opener: Frontier plane hits pedestrian on runway
item.description: A Frontier plane struck a pedestrian on the runway in Denver before takeoff. Meanwhile, the cruise ship with a hantavirus outbreak is set to evacuate passengers on Spain's Canary Islands. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: The Uplift: Desmond Bryant
item.description: David Begnaud sits down with former NFL player Desmond Bryant, who shares how he survived a dark period in his life – and came out helping others.

item.title: U.K.'s Starmer faces calls to resign after disastrous local elections
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged Saturday to revive the Labour Party after a disastrous set of local and regional elections.

item.title: Pedestrian fatally struck by Frontier plane departing Denver for LA
item.description: A pedestrian was hit by a Frontier airplane departing Denver for LA late Friday night, the airport and airline confirmed.

item.title: Audio captures moment Frontier plane fatally struck pedestrian
item.description: The plane was evacuated because of smoke in the aircraft after the collision, according to the flight crew.

item.title: The Love Bombing of Gloria Choi | Post Mortem
item.description: "48 Hours" correspondents Anne-Marie Green and Natalie Morales discuss Gloria Choi's murder. The single mother was shot by her ex-boyfriend, Billy Rickman.

item.title: U.S. awaits Iran's response on peace deal as month-long ceasefire holds
item.description: The U.S. military says its forces fired on two Iran-flagged oil tankers to stop them from docking at an Iranian port in violation of the U.S. blockade in the area.

item.title: A deadly diagnosis almost derailed a mom's dream. Her husband gave her hope.
item.description: Maralee Lellio always dreamed of having a large family. A Stage IV cancer diagnosis almost caused her to lose hope.

item.title: Pentagon says 1 survivor after latest strike on alleged drug boat kills 2
item.description: The U.S. military's latest strike on an alleged drug-trafficking boat in the eastern Pacific Ocean killed two men Friday while leaving one survivor.

item.title: Man who survived hantavirus 24 years ago, but lost mom and sister, recounts experience
item.description: In 2002, Zermeño found out he contracted hantavirus after cleaning the family house following the death of his mother and sister. He had been exposed to rodent droppings and became infected.

item.title: Alabama lawmakers pass plan for new House primary as state pushes to redistrict
item.description: Alabama lawmakers have approved a plan for new House primaries if courts allow the state to use different congressional districts in this year's elections.

item.title: After struggling for years to make ends meet, a daughter opened her home to her mother
item.description: Boca Raton is one of the wealthiest cities in Florida, but even along its golden sands, people still get stuck in fiscal undertows.

item.title: Volcano erupts in Indonesia, killing at least 3 hikers, police say
item.description: Three hikers in an off-limits area died in an eruption of Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano, police said.

item.title: U.S. plans evacuation for Americans on cruise ship in hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The MV Hondius is currently traveling to the Canary Islands, where the 147 people on board will be methodically off-boarded and flown home.

item.title: Pentagon begins releasing UFO files: "It's time the American people see"
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a statement that the documents "have long fueled justified speculation — and it's time the American people see it for themselves."

item.title: 6 videos that stood out in the Pentagon's new UFO drop
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing hundreds of files on UFOs after President Trump ordered the documents to be made public. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson reviewed the 28 videos in the first batch of files and chose six that stood out to her the most.

item.title: Retired Navy fighter pilot who saw UFO urges against racing to conclusions after Pentagon release
item.description: The Department of Defense started releasing previously classified documents related to UFOs on Friday. Lt. Cmdr. Alex Dietrich, a retired Navy fighter pilot who has spoken about her own experience encountering a UFO during a training exercise, joins to discuss.

item.title: House has spent $338,000 since 2004 to settle harassment claims against lawmakers, Rep. Mace claims
item.description: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina says a subpoena revealed that secret payouts used to settle sexual harassment claims against lawmakers have totaled $338,000 since 2004. Former Democratic Rep. Jackie Speier of California joins "The Takeout" with her reaction.

item.title: 5/8: CBS Evening News
item.description: Canary Island residents are concerned about the cruise ship with the hantavirus outbreak; The Pentagon releases UFO files.

item.title: Democrats probe what they call "suspicious circumstances" of some Trump pardons
item.description: Congressional Democrats are investigating whether so-called "pay-to-play" schemes drove some of President Trump's pardons. Democratic Rep. Dave Min of California, one of the three lawmakers who launched the probe, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Fed unlikely to cut interest rates until 2027, Bank of America says
item.description: A "hawkish" turn at the Fed and stubbornly high inflation could delay interest rate cuts, according to Bank of America economists.

item.title: State Department reviewing all Mexican consulates in U.S. as tensions grow
item.description: The State Department is initiating a review of all 53 Mexican consulates in the United States, a U.S. official said, a move that could lead Secretary of State Marco Rubio to consider ordering the closure of some posts.

item.title: U.S. launches major expansion of denaturalization campaign
item.description: The Trump administration announced a major expansion of its denaturalization campaign targeting foreign-born American citizens accused of fraudulently obtaining U.S. citizenship.

item.title: Trump pardon recipients face congressional probe over "pay-to-play" questions
item.description: Congressional Democrats are investigating clemency recipients who may have obtained favorable treatment from Trump or his advisers "through intermediaries, financial contributions, or other forms of influence."

item.title: Mom urges ban on water beads after daughter's injury: "It breaks my heart"
item.description: Water beads may look harmless and fun, but the authors of a new report​ — including a mom whose daughter was seriously injured by them — are calling for a ban​ on their sale.
